name: is_bit_set_not0_no_bound
reason: `is_bit_set(!0usize, j)` is axiomatized for ALL `j: usize` with no `j < 64` guard — for `j >= 64`, `1usize << j` wraps, so the axiom asserts properties about out-of-range bit positions

name: or_bit_set_no_bound
reason: The OR biconditional has no `j < 64` guard — for out-of-range positions where `1usize << j` wraps, this axiom produces conclusions about wrapped bit operations

name: and_bit_set_no_bound
reason: The AND biconditional has no `j < 64` guard — for out-of-range bit positions, `1usize << j` wraps and the axiom asserts equivalences that don't hold for real bit semantics

name: lemma_view_no_range_guard
reason: `lemma_view`'s first ensures decomposes any `i` in `self@` without guarding `0 <= a < 8` — for `i >= 512` or `i < 0`, `a` falls outside `[0,8)` making `self.mask[a]` an out-of-bounds spec access

name: not0_and_zero_inconsistency
reason: Combining unbounded `is_bit_set(!0, j)` with unbounded AND biconditional yields `is_bit_set(0, j)` for `j >= 64` — but `is_bit_set(0, j)` should be false, exposing a potential inconsistency in the axiom system at out-of-range positions
